findings.fix_prompt: On Sparkle's sign-in page, rip out the "Dev Bypass (Skip Login)" button, the "Choose Your Dev Role" modal, and any "For development purposes only" copy. Only show that stuff when import.meta.env.DEV is true. Nothing dev-facing on the published lovable.app URL.
findings.title: Dev bypass on the live sign-in page
findings.what_we_saw: On the public sign-in screen there's a "Dev Bypass (Skip Login)" button. Tap it, pick a role, and you're in. No password.
findings.why_it_matters: On a marketplace that's basically an open door. Strangers can wander in as a host or buyer.

findings.fix_prompt: On Sparkle's signed-in homepage, delete the "Developer Tools" / "Development Data Seeder" block (Seed Sample Data, Clear My Data). If you still need it locally, wrap the whole section in import.meta.env.DEV so it never shows on prod.
findings.title: Developer Tools card on the homepage everyone sees
findings.what_we_saw: Right after login the homepage has a "Developer Tools" card with "Seed Sample Data" and "Clear My Data." It's on the live URL, not tucked behind a dev flag.
findings.why_it_matters: Reads like a beta sticker on an otherwise polished app. Investors and paying users notice.

findings.fix_prompt: Add real /privacy and /terms pages for Sparkle Marketplace. Link them from the footer on every page. Drop in your real support email. Match the fonts and spacing you already use.
findings.title: Privacy and Terms in the footer hit 404
findings.what_we_saw: Footer links to privacy and terms. Both URLs just show Sparkle's 404 page.
findings.why_it_matters: Stripe, app reviewers, and anyone with a budget will look for these before they trust you with money or data.

findings.fix_prompt: Either wire up Quick Book so slots actually show, or hide the button when there are none and say something like "No dates yet, check back soon." Don't leave a dead modal.
findings.title: Quick Book goes nowhere
findings.what_we_saw: The big "Quick Book" CTA opens a modal that says "No available slots found." Book Now stays greyed out. No other way to book.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the main thing the app is for. People who try to book just bounce. Most won't email you about it.

findings.fix_prompt: On Sparkle's Quick Book modal: cap the height to the viewport, let the slot list scroll inside the modal, and keep "Book Now" visible above the iPhone safe area. Sanity-check at 390px and iPhone SE in Chrome DevTools.
findings.title: Booking modal on mobile: button below the fold
findings.what_we_saw: On a phone the Quick Book modal is taller than the screen. Time picker and Book Now sit under the fold and it's not obvious you can scroll.
findings.why_it_matters: Most launches get more than half their traffic on phones. Broken booking on mobile is lost money you never hear about.

findings.fix_prompt: On listing cards: if review count is 0, show "New" or "No reviews yet" instead of a star number. Only show a rating once there's at least one real review.
findings.title: 4.5 stars with zero reviews
findings.what_we_saw: Experience cards say "4.5 (0)." Looks like demo data.
findings.why_it_matters: Buyers clock fake social proof fast. Makes real hosts look worse too.

findings.fix_prompt: Pull each experience's real host name from the database for the "Hosted by" line. If you don't have one yet, use first name + last initial or hide the line. Kill the "Hosted by Local Host" placeholder everywhere.
findings.title: Every listing still says Hosted by Local Host
findings.what_we_saw: Same placeholder host name on every card. No real people.
findings.why_it_matters: Marketplaces live on trusting who's behind the listing. This screams "inventory isn't real yet."

passed_checks: Search, filters, and listing pages feel like a real product
Branding and layout are ahead of most pre-launch Lovable apps
Auth and nav work fine once you strip the dev-only UI

share_metadata.description: Sample Starter audit for Sparkle Marketplace: dev tools on prod, dead booking, mobile modal issues, and a few trust gaps you'd fix before sharing the link.
share_metadata.title: LaunchLook audit for Sparkle Marketplace

verdict.label: Needs fixes before launch
verdict.narrative: Sparkle looks investor-ready in screenshots. Then you click through. Dev shortcuts are still on the live URL, legal pages 404, Quick Book goes nowhere, and on a phone the booking modal is a pain. None of this needs a redesign: yank the two critical dev panels, fix booking, then you're in decent shape to share the link. The rest is trust polish for an afternoon.
verdict.summary: Pretty shell, a few things I'd fix before posting the URL anywhere.
